_doc: Function-name-keyed acknowledged sites for /pre-mortem (G-PM5/G-PM6, S246 ES-7). Line numbers drift on every refactor; function names are the stable key. Update THIS FILE with code changes, not skill prose. VERIFY against current state before trusting — a stale 'known gap' teaches the operator to disbelieve the scan (the G-PM1 lesson). Consumed by scripts/preMortemScan.js.

_scanExcludeFiles_reason: Meta/detector files whose DATA legitimately contains 'Math.random' as patterns-to-detect (V2_DEPRECATED_PATTERNS) or throw-if-invoked guards (v3Random_, zero callers). _neighborhoodScanScopeNote: scanNeighborhoods() (preMortemScan.js) iterates PHASE_DIRS ONLY — scripts/ + utilities/ are out of scope by design (preMortemScan.js:196-198). S247 ES-7 follow-up: the exhaustive (scripts-included) sweep's ~10 extra literals (East Oakland/Elmhurst/Jingletown/Coliseum/Brooklyn in buildInitiativePackets, editionIntake/V3, registerEchoRoster, buildCivicVoicePackets, djDirect, prepAthleteIntegration, photoQA) were triaged and are ALL benign: DJ photo-canon (Coliseum = the A's ballpark), citizen-backstory hometowns, civic D5 district geography, and edition-recognition whitelists — NONE write a non-canon neighborhood to Simulation_Ledger. Not added to acknowledgedNeighborhoodLiterals below because that list is matched only against PHASE_DIRS files (ack.has(rel+'|'+literal)); scripts/ entries would be dead config. Re-triage only if the scan scope ever expands past PHASE_DIRS. (The 229-row ledger neighborhood drift is a SEPARATE data-cleanup item, not a code-literal issue.)
